dashboard.eodPrompt: What are your top 3 priorities for tomorrow?
dashboard.pressEscapeHint: Press Escape or click to exit

kanban.dragHint: Drag cards between columns to move jobs.
kanban.irreversibleTooltip: Irreversible — jobs cannot move backward from this stage
kanban.explodeBomConfirmMessage: This will create sub-jobs for all "Make" items in the BOM of part {{partNumber}}. This action cannot be undone.
kanban.archiveJobsMessage: This will archive {{count}} selected job(s). You can restore them later.

backlog.title: Backlog
backlog.subtitle: Unscheduled jobs waiting to be planned
backlog.noJobsHelp: Try adjusting your search or filters. Jobs are added from the Board page.
backlog.unarchiveMessage: Job {{jobNumber}} will be restored to the active list.
backlog.unarchiveJobsMessage: {{count}} archived job(s) will be restored to the active list.

parts.title: Parts Catalog

draftIndicator.label: Draft
draftIndicator.tooltip: Workflow in progress — current step: {{step}}. Click to resume.

drafts.abandonConfirmMessage: The in-progress workflow will be discarded. This cannot be undone.

parts.subtitle: Manage parts, BOMs, and specifications
parts.noPartsHelp: Create your first part to start building your catalog. Parts can include BOMs, operations, and file attachments.
parts.notLinked: Not linked to any accounting item
parts.unlinkMessage: This will remove the link between "{partNumber}" and its accounting item.
parts.deleteBomMessage: This will permanently remove this BOM entry from the part.
parts.bomRevisionHistoryHint: Each component change creates an immutable snapshot.
parts.bomRevisionHistoryEmptyHelp: Adding a BOM line will create revision 1.
parts.bomRevisionEmptySnapshot: This revision contains no component lines.
parts.bomAtReleaseStale: BOM has been updated since this Job was released.
parts.bomAtReleaseNone: Not pinned to a BOM revision.
parts.deleteOperationMessage: This will remove operation {{stepNumber}}: "{{title}}" from the routing.
parts.noMaterials: No materials assigned to this operation
parts.noMaterialsHint: Add BOM entries to this part first, then assign them to operations.

workflow.abandonFailed: Couldn't abandon the draft. Try again.

fork.title: How would you like to add this part?
fork.step1MakeDesc: Produced in-house from raw stock or a BOM.
fork.step1SubcontractDesc: Vendor builds the entire part on our behalf.
fork.step2RawDesc: Bulk input — bar stock, sheet, pellets, wire.
fork.step2ComponentDesc: Single-piece part used in a parent BOM.
fork.step2SubassemblyDesc: Multi-level build used in higher assemblies.
fork.step3Helper: Optional descriptive tag — Fastener, Electronic, Packaging, etc. Skip if unsure.
fork.step4Title: How would you like to fill it in?
fork.step4GuidedDesc: Walk through each cluster of fields in order.

violations.procurementRequired: Pick how this part is sourced

basics.title: Basics
basics.hint: Name and a short description — the minimum to identify this part. The procurement and inventory axes were chosen on the previous screen.
basics.rationale:
Identity is everything else's foundation. The part number + name resolve every reference downstream — POs, work orders, BOM positions, ledger lines.

Skipping basics is impossible: no part exists until this step saves. The procurement source + inventory class chosen on the previous screen drive which steps follow this one and which gates fire at promotion time.

bom.title: Bill of Materials
bom.hint: List every component this assembly uses. You'll need at least one entry to continue.
bom.emptyHelp: Add components from the parts catalog to define this assembly's BOM.
bom.rationale:
The BOM is the recipe. Without it, MRP can't explode demand into component requirements, cost roll-up has nothing to sum, and shop orders can't issue material.

Each row is a child part + quantity (with optional scrap factor). For Make + Subassembly / FinishedGood combos this step is required for promotion; for Make + Component it's optional (single-piece fab from raw stock is valid).

routing.title: Routing
routing.hint: Define the operation steps to manufacture this part.
routing.rationale:
Routing is the build sequence. Operations + work centers + cycle/setup times feed work-order generation, capacity planning, labor cost roll-up, and shop-floor traveler printing.

Subcontract operations (mark a step as outsourced) automatically generate vendor POs at the right point in the sequence — no separate PO creation needed.

costing.title: Cost
costing.hint: Set how this part's cost is calculated and (for Tier 1) the manual price.
costing.modeFlatDesc: Single fixed amount entered by you. Use until you have data for tiered costing.
costing.modeDepartmentalDesc: Per-cost-center percentage applied based on routing.
costing.modeAbcDesc: Cost pools allocated by drivers (square footage, machine hours, etc.).
costing.tier2Tooltip: Enable CAP-COSTING-TIER2-DEPTRATES in admin to use
costing.tier3Tooltip: Enable CAP-COSTING-TIER3-ABC in admin to use
costing.tierNotImplemented: Capability is enabled but the calculation engine + configuration screens are still pending. Tier choice is stored on the part for future use; the manual override below remains the active value.
costing.rationale:
Cost drives every dollar downstream. Quote pricing, margin reporting, WIP valuation, and standard-vs-actual variance analysis all read from this number.

Tier 1 (manual override) is the simplest path: enter a number you trust. Tier 2 / Tier 3 add per-cost-center rates and activity-based pools — capability-gated and not implemented yet, so today the manual override is the working answer.

alternates.title: Alternates
alternates.hint: Optional — list substitute or equivalent parts for procurement flexibility.

express.title: Quick add
express.hint: Fill in everything at once. Switch to step-by-step from the header anytime.
express.typeLockedHint: Set when the part was created. Edit on the part detail page if you need to change it.

promote.hint: Runs readiness checks. Tells you what's missing if anything blocks promotion.
promote.missingShort: Some readiness gates aren't satisfied yet.

sourcing.title: Preferred Vendor
sourcing.hint: Pick the default vendor for this part. Lead time, MOQ, pack size, OEM identity, and pricing are entered per-vendor on the next step.
sourcing.rationale:
The preferred vendor is the default for new POs and RFQs. Buyers can override per-PO, but everything assumes this is the first call.

Without a preferred vendor, automated PO generation, MRP-driven planned orders, and lead-time-aware scheduling all stall — they need someone to ask. Backup vendors + tier pricing + per-vendor MOQ live on the next step.

manufacturer.title: Manufacturer
manufacturer.hint: Engineering OEM identity — distinct from any distributor we buy through.

inventory.title: Inventory
inventory.hint: Stock thresholds and unit of measure. Drives reorder triggers and bin defaults.
inventory.defaultBinHelper: Numeric bin id. Replaced by a real bin picker in a later release.
inventory.rationale:
Stock UoM is the unit every other inventory transaction uses. Receipts, issues, transfers, counts — all denominated here. Pick wrong and conversions ripple through every downstream report.

Min / reorder / safety-stock are policy: they drive when MRP fires planned orders or when manual reorder reports highlight a part. Defaults okay if you don't know yet — adjust later as demand patterns emerge.

quality.title: Quality
quality.hint: Receiving inspection, traceability, ABC class, hazmat, and shelf life.
quality.rationale:
Traceability mode is irreversible-ish — you can change it later but every prior transaction was recorded against the old setting. Lot or serial unlocks per-lot recall, expiration tracking, and per-unit warranty / RMA workflows. Bulk skips all of that.

ABC class drives cycle-counting cadence (A counted weekly, C yearly, etc.) and prioritization in stockout-resolution reports. Hazmat + shelf life only matter if regulated-industry capabilities are on for this install.

shipping.title: Shipping & Physical
shipping.hint: Mass, dimensions, and volume. Drives shipping rate quotes and inventory cube.

toolAsset.title: Tooling Asset
toolAsset.hint: Link this part to its tooling Asset record (for cavity, life, calibration tracking).
toolAsset.moldToolRefHelper: Vestigial — use Tooling Asset above when possible.

sourcePart.title: Source Part
sourcePart.hint: The pre-finishing in-house part that's sent to the subcontractor.
sourcePart.sourcePartHelper: The pre-finishing in-house part that's sent to the vendor.

vendor.title: Subcontract Vendor
vendor.hint: Pick the subcontract vendor for this part. Lead time and per-vendor terms are entered on the next step.

vendorParts.title: Vendor Sources
vendorParts.hint: Each vendor that supplies this part is its own group below — vendor-specific identifiers, lead time, MOQ, and price tiers. The preferred vendor (set on the previous step) shows first; add alternates as needed.
vendorParts.pendingMaterialization: Save earlier steps first to enable vendor sources for this part.
vendorParts.rationale:
Vendor Sources is where pricing and per-vendor terms live. Purchase Order creation reads price tiers from here — without at least one tier on the chosen vendor, the system can't quote a price for the requested quantity and the PO line will block.

The preferred vendor (set on the prior step) is the default for new POs; you can override per-PO. Adding alternate vendors here gives buyers fallback options when the preferred is out of stock or over-MOQ for the situation.

flags.title: Flags
flags.hint: Phantom-specific flags: kit, configurator, and backflush policy.
flags.isConfigurableHelper: Enables the configurator wizard for this parent.

salesHooks.title: Sales Setup
salesHooks.hint: Sales-side parameters for resold finished goods.
salesHooks.defaultSalesPriceHelper: Editable on the Pricing tab (coming soon).
salesHooks.inferredPriceSourcePriceListEntry: From price-list entry for current customer

detail.sourcesEmptyHelp: Add a vendor that supplies this part to track lead time, MOQ, and pricing.

tabs.placeholderHelp: This tab is coming in Pillar 4 Phase 2.

purchaseHistory.empty: No purchase orders include this part yet.

material.title: Material & Physical
material.specHelper: Engineering material spec — falls back to free-text Material when not set.

uom.title: Units of Measure
uom.helper: Stock = inventory unit. Purchase = vendor invoice unit. Sales = customer invoice unit.

mrp.title: MRP Planning
mrp.leadTimeNote: Lead time resolves from preferred VendorPart when configured.

pricing.notesHelper: Why the price changed (e.g. "competitor matched", "raw cost up").
pricing.closedRowHelper: Closed (superseded by a later effective row)
pricing.confirmDelete: Remove this price row from history?

landedCost.title: Landed Cost
landedCost.empty: No receipts with captured freight yet — landed cost will populate once you receive a PO and record actual freight.

vendorSources.stubHint: Fill in details to add this source
vendorSources.duplicate: That vendor is already a source for this part.
vendorSources.noTiersInline: No price tiers on this source yet.

empty.message: No pricing tiers across any vendor source.
empty.help: Add a vendor source and a price tier to see consolidated pricing here.

inspector.label: Selected vendor source details
inspector.placeholder: Select a vendor source on the left to see and edit its details here.

removeTier.confirmMessage: Removing the tier closes it for any future PO that would have read this price. Existing POs already in flight are unaffected.

removeRow.confirmMessage: Removes {{vendor}} as a source for this part, including its lead time, MOQ, and any price tiers. Existing POs already in flight are unaffected.

empty.help: Add a vendor that supplies this part to track lead time, MOQ, and pricing.
empty.helpReadonly: Click Edit to add a vendor source for this part.

vendorPart.vendorMpnHelper: OEM identity as carried by this vendor. Different distributors of the same part can list different manufacturer names + MPNs.
vendorPart.partNumberWhenMfrHelper: The vendor's catalog number IS the manufacturer part number when buying direct from the OEM.
vendorPart.isManufacturerHelper: Tick when buying direct from the OEM (not a distributor). Manufacturer name is taken from the vendor; one Part # field replaces the separate vendor SKU and MPN.

bulkImport.helper: Upload a CSV with columns: partNumber (required), vendorPartNumber, manufacturerName, vendorMpn, leadTimeDays, minOrderQty, packSize, countryOfOrigin, htsCode, notes (all optional).
bulkImport.previewEmpty: No rows in the uploaded file.
bulkImport.applyDisabledHelper: Fix the errored rows in your CSV and re-upload.
bulkImport.previewFailed: Failed to read the CSV file.

vendorPart.countryOfOriginHelper: ISO-3166 alpha-2 code, e.g. US / CN / DE
vendorPart.isPreferredHelper: Only one vendor per part can be the preferred source.

priceTiers.title: Price Tiers
priceTiers.empty: No price tiers — add one to enable per-quantity pricing.

inventory.title: Inventory
inventory.subtitle: Track stock levels, locations, and movements
inventory.lowStockBannerSingular: part is below minimum stock level
inventory.lowStockBannerPlural: parts are below minimum stock level
inventory.noInventoryDataHelp: Stock appears here when parts are received into bin locations. Use the Receiving tab to log incoming goods.
inventory.selectLocationToView: Select a location to view details
inventory.adjustStockDesc: Manually adjust quantity for a bin content

customers.title: Customers
customers.subtitle: Manage customer contacts and addresses
customers.quickAddHint: Just the essentials. Add credit terms, tax, and addresses later from the customer page — or use Guided setup for the full record.

contactsPage.title: All Contacts
contactsPage.subtitle: Flat cross-customer view of every contact — "who do I know at company X?".
contactsPage.empty: No contacts yet. Add contacts to customers to populate this view.

portalAccessPage.title: Portal Access
portalAccessPage.subtitle: Admin oversight of which contacts can sign in to the customer portal.
portalAccessPage.empty: No contacts have portal access yet. Click "Provision Access" above to pick a contact.
portalAccessPage.provisionHint: Pick a contact to grant portal access. Only contacts with an email address (the portal login identifier) and no existing access appear here.
portalAccessPage.provisionNoneEligible: No eligible contacts. Every contact either lacks an email or already has portal access.

segmentsPage.title: Segments
segmentsPage.subtitle: Saved cross-customer filter sets for marketing + reporting.
segmentsPage.placeholderHelp: Define a filter (e.g. "open AR > $10K AND industry = aerospace AND last order < 90 days"), save it as a named segment, and reuse across reports, marketing campaigns, and dashboards.

aerospaceHighValue.name: High-Value Aerospace
aerospaceHighValue.description: AS9100-certified customers with > $10K open AR. Useful for the quarterly account-review cadence.

itarReady.name: ITAR-cleared Reference Pool
itarReady.description: Defense customers cleared for ITAR work who have also opted in as references for similar prospects.

dormant90.name: Dormant > 90 Days
dormant90.description: Active customers with no order in the last quarter. Re-engagement campaign target.

creditWatch.name: Credit Watch List
creditWatch.description: Customers on credit hold or with utilization above 80%. Surfaces in the AR aging review.

importPage.title: Bulk Import
importPage.subtitle: Upload a CSV of customers + contacts, dedup against existing rows.
importPage.placeholderHelp: Same dedup-wizard pattern as Leads bulk intake — upload CSV, preview matches, gate quality before commit. Differs in that customers carry billing/shipping addresses + credit terms + contacts as a hierarchy.

upload.title: Upload CSV
upload.description: Drag-and-drop or browse to select a CSV. Header row required; column aliases auto-detected.

dedupe.title: Match against existing rows
dedupe.description: Server runs the same dedupe pipeline as leads bulk intake — by email, then phone, then companyName + state.

review.title: Review per-row status
review.description: Each row gets a chip: Created / DuplicateExisting / Invalid / MissingRequiredField. Operator chooses to skip or override.

commit.title: Commit
commit.description: Inserts the Created rows in a single transaction. Address + contact hierarchy is built atomically alongside each customer.

fork.title: How would you like to add this customer?
fork.hint: Pick the path that matches what you already know about this account.
fork.quickDesc: Drop in a name + a couple of contact bits and keep moving.
fork.fromLeadDesc: Pick an existing lead and run it through the convert stepper.
fork.guidedDesc: Step through identity, engagement, addresses, and billing for a strategic account.

leadPicker.title: Pick a lead to convert
leadPicker.hint: Only leads that aren't yet Lost or Converted appear here.
leadPicker.search: Search by company, contact, or email

guided.title: Guided customer setup
guided.step1Hint: Who is this customer? Capture the basics first.
guided.step2Hint: What kind of relationship are we setting up? Pick the closest fit.
guided.step3Hint: Billing first; toggle off "same as billing" to capture a separate ship-to.
guided.step4Hint: Optional today — set a credit limit and tax-exempt status if you have them.
guided.step5Hint: Confirm and create. You can edit anything from the customer detail page after.

customers.deleteCustomerMessage: This will remove "{name}" from the system. This action cannot be undone.

tabs.placeholderHelp: This tab is coming in a follow-up dispatch.

creditStatus.title: Credit Status
creditStatus.placeHoldDialogHint: Customer will be blocked from new orders while on hold.

identityCluster.nameImmutabilityHint: Identity field — changing this affects search, cross-entity references, and accounting links.

compliance.title: Regulated Industries
compliance.hint: Toggles flag this customer for downstream QA / documentation requirements. Each can be set independently.
compliance.fdaRegulatedHelp: Medical device or pharma. Triggers QA review of capability fit on every related lead.
compliance.aerospaceHelp: Aerospace certified work. Affects part documentation requirements.
compliance.automotiveHelp: Automotive supplier. Drives PPAP requirements + traceability rigor.
compliance.itarControlledHelp: Export-controlled defense work. Gates ITAR clearance on every related lead.
compliance.referenceHint: When enabled, this customer can be used as a reference for prospects in their industry.
compliance.referenceOkHelp: Sales reps can cite this customer when building rapport with similar prospects.
compliance.referenceNotesEmpty: No notes — add scope details (e.g. "ok for aerospace prospects only, no defense").

tabs.noJobsYet: No jobs linked to this customer

estimates.title: Title
estimates.convertMessage: Convert "{{title}}" to a formal quote? The estimate will be marked as Accepted.

pricing.emptyHelp: Add a part to start pricing this list.

priceListEntry.minQuantityHelper: Quantity break — applies for orders ≥ this size.
priceListEntry.cellEditError: Couldn't save — press Esc to cancel

bulkImport.label: Import CSV
bulkImport.helper: Upload a CSV with columns: partNumber, unitPrice, minQuantity (optional), currency (optional), notes (optional).
bulkImport.previewEmpty: No rows in the uploaded file.
bulkImport.applyDisabledHelper: Fix the errored rows in your CSV and re-upload.
bulkImport.previewFailed: Failed to read the CSV file.

priceList.effectiveFromHelper: When this list starts being valid for orders. Leave blank for 'always-on'.
priceList.effectiveToHelper: When this list expires. Leave blank for 'no expiry'.
priceList.isDefaultHelper: Apply this list when no other rule matches. Only one default per customer.
priceList.isActiveHelper: Inactive lists don't apply to new orders.

leads.title: Leads

intake.title: Bulk Intake
intake.subtitle: Upload a list, dedup against existing leads + suppression, gate quality before commit.
intake.placeholderMessage: Upload a list to get started
intake.placeholderHelp: Upload a CSV or paste rows below. The dedup wizard previews matches against existing leads, suppressed contacts, and active cooldowns before any rows commit.
intake.strategyHint: The strategy drives which fields are required and which suppression channel is checked. Cold-call needs phone; cold-email needs email; trade-show follow-up accepts either.
intake.uploadHint: Header row required. Recognized columns: companyName, contactName, email, phone, source, notes (case-insensitive, common variants accepted).
intake.columnsHelpHint: Column matching is case-insensitive and the listed aliases all map to the same field. Only `companyName` is required; the rest can be left blank without blocking the upload.
intake.pasteLabel: Paste rows (CSV or tab-separated, header on first line)
intake.previewHint: Run the preview to see which rows will be created, suppressed, or skipped. No leads are created until you click commit.

queue.title: Worker Queue
queue.subtitle: Pull-based queue for high-volume outreach. Single-keystroke dispositions, J/K to advance.
queue.readyHelp: Pull a slice of leads and the queue serves them one at a time. Two reps pulling at the same time get disjoint slices — no overlap.
queue.empty: No more leads to pull right now. Check back later or pick a different campaign.
queue.dialFailed: Couldn't place the call. Check the phone number format.
queue.placeholderHelp: Pull leads from the queue to start working through them. Single-keystroke dispositions advance to the next lead automatically.
queue.shortcutHint: Tap a highlighted key to fire the matching action. J/K move between leads in this batch.

campaigns.title: Campaigns
campaigns.subtitle: Wrap each bulk batch in a Campaign for cohort reporting and per-strategy config.
campaigns.placeholderMessage: No campaigns yet. Create one to track bulk-marketing cohorts.
campaigns.placeholderHelp: A Campaign carries the strategy type, channel, required fields, owner, dates, and default cooldown override. Every Lead from a bulk import gets a CampaignId FK so reporting can answer "September LinkedIn batch vs August" cleanly.

suppression.title: Suppression / Do Not Contact
suppression.subtitle: Manage per-channel opt-outs and active cooldown windows.
suppression.empty: No suppressed leads. New opt-outs and cooldowns appear here as they accumulate.

accounts.title: Accounts
accounts.hint: Multi-contact B2B parent groupings. Each lead optionally points at one of these so a single prospect's engineering/procurement/exec sponsor contacts roll up together.
accounts.empty: No accounts yet. Create one to group leads sharing a company.
accounts.deleteMessage: Soft-delete "{{ name }}"? Already-converted leads are unaffected; the account itself is removed from this list.
accounts.deleteBlockedMessage: "{{ name }}" still has {{ count }} lead(s) linked to it. Unassign the account on those leads first, then retry.

samples.title: Sample Shipments
samples.hint: Pre-quote sample parts in flight — manage status from the lead detail panel.
samples.empty: No sample shipments yet. Sales reps request samples from a lead's detail panel.

leads.bulkAssignHint: {{ count }} lead(s) will be linked to the picked account. Pick "— None —" to unaffiliate.
leads.bulkAssignPartial: Assigned {{ ok }} of {{ total }} — some failed. Check the activity log for details.

fork.title: How would you like to add this lead?
fork.step1Title: What kind of opportunity is this?
fork.step1Hint: Pick the shape that best matches the sales motion. "Quick add" lands the basics fast — the others surface a few extra fields tailored to that motion (RFQ details, decision-maker context, etc.).
fork.step2Hint: Fields with a *  are required. Optional fields can be filled in later from the lead detail.
fork.shapeUnknownDesc: Just the basics — company, contact, source. Skip the extras and tailor later.
fork.shapeQuickQuoteDesc: Inbound request with a formed need. Capture parts list, target due date, target price.
fork.shapeRepeatDesc: Existing customer-shaped lead. Reference the prior job and pricing context.
fork.shapeStrategicDesc: Long sales cycle, multiple stakeholders. Capture decision-maker + champion + current vendor.
fork.shapePrototypeDesc: Custom or exploratory. Capture project type, expected timeline, capability fit notes.
fork.rfqPartsHint: List the part numbers and quantities they're asking about. One per line.
fork.rfqTargetPriceHint: What they hinted at, or your read of where the deal works.
fork.decisionMakerHint: Who signs off — title or name if you know it.
fork.championHint: Who inside the org wants this to happen.
fork.currentVendorHint: Who's getting the work today, if anyone.
fork.referenceJobHint: Prior job number, PO, or work order this should reference.
fork.projectTypeHint: One-off prototype, batch run, design-build, etc.
fork.expectedTimelineHint: Weeks to first piece, or however the customer phrased it.

leads.subtitle: Track and qualify potential sales opportunities
leads.noLeadsHelp: Leads track potential sales opportunities. Click New Lead to start capturing prospects.
leads.convertedInfo: This lead has been converted to a customer.

playbook.QuickQuote: Turn the quote around fast — the buyer is in price-comparison mode. Aim for a same-day response.
playbook.Repeat: Reference the prior job. Pricing context and lead time consistency matter most.
playbook.Strategic: Map decision-makers and the champion. Plan a multi-touch cadence; expect a longer cycle.
playbook.Prototype: Confirm capability fit early. Set expectations on iteration count and timeline.
playbook.Unknown: Capture context as you learn it — the shape can be reclassified once the motion is clearer.

engagement.staleTooltip: No activity in the last 14 days — consider a follow-up or reclassify.

classification.title: Classification
classification.campaignTooltip: Bulk-marketing campaign that produced this lead. Set at intake.
classification.campaignNavTooltip: Open this campaign in the Campaigns page.

leads.convertMessage: Convert "{{name}}" to a customer? You can also create a job for the new customer.
leads.convertedWithJob: Lead converted to customer and job created.

convertStepper.title: Convert to Customer
convertStepper.step1Hint: These fields come from the lead. They become the new customer's identity.
convertStepper.accountRolloverHint: {{ count }} contact(s) from this account will also be added to the new Customer as non-primary contacts. The lead's own contact (above) becomes primary.
convertStepper.step2Hint: Fill in the fields a customer needs that a lead doesn't. All optional — skip anything you'll fill in later.
convertStepper.step2SameAsBilling: Shipping address is the same as billing
convertStepper.step2CreateJob: Also create a job after conversion
convertStepper.step3Hint: Review and convert. The lead will be marked as Converted and the new customer will appear in the customer list.
convertStepper.step3JobToggle: A new job will be created in the default track.
convertStepper.step3NoCustomerDetails: No additional customer details — you can fill these in on the customer detail page after conversion.

violations.taxExemptionIdRequired: Tax-exempt customers need an exemption certificate ID.

expenses.title: Expenses
expenses.subtitle: Log expenses, attach receipts, and track approvals
expenses.noExpensesHelp: Log business expenses here. Attach receipts and link to jobs for accurate cost tracking.
expenses.deleteMessage: This will permanently delete this expense record.
expenses.declineNoteHint: Note required for Reject / Request Revision — minimum {{ min }} chars ({{ len }}).
expenses.declineNoteRequiredTooltip: A note of at least {{ min }} characters is required to reject or request revision.
expenses.noUpcomingExpenses: No upcoming expenses in the next 90 days
expenses.deleteRecurringMessage: This will remove "{{name}}" from recurring expenses.
expenses.receiptRequiredHint: A receipt is required for this expense before it can be submitted.

timeTracking.title: Time Tracking
timeTracking.subtitle: Track hours with live timers or manual entries
timeTracking.lockedTitle: Locked — past day or manually locked
timeTracking.deleteMessage: This will permanently delete this time entry.

assets.title: Assets
assets.subtitle: Track equipment, tooling, and company property
assets.name: Name

admin.title: Admin Settings
admin.subtitle: Users, track types, reference data, and system configuration
admin.setupCodeHint: Give this code to the employee. They enter it at the login screen to set their password & PIN.
admin.accountNotSetUpHint: This user hasn't completed registration. Generate a setup code for them.
admin.rfidHint: Tap and hold card on reader — it may take a moment to register
admin.label: Label
admin.logoDescription: Upload a logo to replace the default text mark in the header (max 5MB, image files)
admin.brandLockupsDescription: Replace the Forge lockups shown on the login screen, app header, and browser tab. Upload a PNG, SVG, or .ico; reset any one back to the bundled default at any time.
admin.lockupMarqueeHint: Shown on the login and setup screens
admin.lockupWordmarkHint: Shown in the app header and about dialog
admin.userSavedLocationFailed: User saved but failed to update work location
admin.userCreated: User created — share the setup code with {{name}}
admin.deleteTrackTypeMessage: This will deactivate "{{name}}" and all its stages. Existing jobs will remain.
admin.deleteLocationMessage: This will deactivate "{{name}}". Users assigned here will fall back to the default location.
admin.fileUploadedPayStub: File uploaded — fill in pay stub details to save
admin.fileUploadedTaxDoc: File uploaded — fill in tax document details to save
admin.uploadAndFillFields: Please upload a file and fill in all fields
admin.lockPeriodHint: Lock a pay period to prevent further edits

leadSources.title: Lead Sources
leadSources.hint: Catalog of formal lead-attribution sources. Quality scores are computed nightly from disposition outcomes on linked leads — high score means the source converts; low score means the list provider delivers garbage.
leadSources.empty: No lead sources yet. Add at least one before bulk intake to get clean attribution.
leadSources.namePlaceholder: e.g. Web form, Trade show 2026, ZoomInfo export
leadSources.deleteMessage: Soft-delete "{{name}}"? If any lead is attributed to this source, the server will refuse — deactivate instead.

icpRubrics.title: ICP Rubrics
icpRubrics.hint: Ideal-customer-profile scoring schemes. The default rubric drives the nightly LeadScore recompute; dimensions match lead/customer fields and contribute their weight to the total.
icpRubrics.empty: No ICP rubrics yet. Add one and mark it default to start scoring leads.
icpRubrics.dimensionsEmpty: No dimensions defined yet — add at least one to score against.
icpRubrics.deleteMessage: Soft-delete "{{name}}"? Cannot delete the default rubric — mark another rubric default first.

assignmentRules.title: Lead Assignment Rules
assignmentRules.hint: Priority-ordered rules that route incoming leads to reps. First match wins; lower priority number evaluates first.
assignmentRules.empty: No assignment rules yet. Leads will fall through to the open queue until at least one rule is configured.
assignmentRules.specRepIdsHint: Comma-separated user IDs of reps to rotate among. Empty rule never matches.
assignmentRules.specTerritoryHint: Lead matches when its state is in the list OR its ZIP starts with one of the prefixes. At least one of the two must be set.
assignmentRules.specIndustriesHint: Lead matches when its account.industry value is in this comma-separated list (case-insensitive).
assignmentRules.specAccountBasedHint: Account-based rules need no extra config. They match when the lead's company already has an owner via a related lead/customer and sticky-assign to that owner.
assignmentRules.rawSpecHint: Used when the structured fields above are empty. Lets you hand-craft a spec for kinds the form doesn't yet surface.
assignmentRules.deleteMessage: Soft-delete "{{name}}"? Leads previously assigned via this rule keep their assignment — only future evaluations are affected.

currencies.title: Currencies & Exchange Rates
currencies.currenciesHint: Catalog of currencies the install accepts. Mark exactly one base currency.
currencies.currenciesEmpty: No currencies configured yet. Add at least the base currency before recording transactions.
currencies.ratesHint: Append-only history of per-pair, per-day FX rates. Most recent rate per pair wins at conversion time.
currencies.ratesEmpty: No exchange rates recorded yet. Set at least one pair to enable currency conversion.
currencies.ratePlaceholder: 1 unit of From = N units of To
currencies.needTwoCurrencies: Add at least two active currencies before setting an exchange rate.

tariffs.title: Tariff Rates
tariffs.hint: HTS-code import duty rates feed the landed-cost duty component on the part Cost tab. SCD-2: to retire a rate, edit the row and set Effective To, then add a new row with a fresh Effective From.
tariffs.empty: No tariff rates yet — landed cost will show duty as "—" until rates are imported.
tariffs.deleteMessage: Soft-delete the {{hts}} / {{country}} rate? Landed-cost duty for receipts using this rate will fall back to the next-effective row (or 0% if none).

entityCompleteness.title: Entity Completeness Requirements
entityCompleteness.subtitle: Define which entity fields each capability requires.

confirmDelete.title: Delete requirement?
confirmDelete.message: This will permanently remove requirement "{{requirementId}}". The completeness chip will refresh on next render.

teamsPanel.noTeamsHelp: Create a team to group workers for kiosk clock-in stations
teamsPanel.memberHint: Select users to assign to this team. A user can only belong to one team at a time.
teamsPanel.deleteFailed: Failed to delete team — check for active terminals
teamsPanel.removeTerminalConfirm: Remove terminal "{{name}}"? Any kiosk connected on this terminal will lose its session immediately.

onboarding.title: New Employee Onboarding
onboarding.subtitle: Complete all steps to finish your onboarding paperwork.
onboarding.completeMessage: Your forms have been submitted and sent for signing. Your employer will review your I-9 identity documents within 3 business days of your start date. You'll receive email confirmations as each document is processed.
onboarding.reviewSubtitle: Review each pre-filled form, then sign it. You can go back to edit if anything looks wrong.
onboarding.previewWarning: This is how your form will be filled. If anything is wrong, go back and correct it before signing.
onboarding.noPdfPreview: No PDF preview is available for this form. Review the details below and proceed to sign.
onboarding.signInstruction: Complete all required fields and apply your signature below.

filingStatuses.marriedJoint: Married filing jointly or Qualifying surviving spouse

citizenshipStatuses.citizen: A citizen of the United States
citizenshipStatuses.noncitizen: A noncitizen national of the United States

onboarding.completeSignatureNote: Complete your signature above to continue to the next form.
onboarding.listBCDocProvided: List B + List C documents provided

personal.title: Personal Information
personal.desc: This information is used to pre-fill your W-4, I-9, and state withholding forms. Enter it once here — you won't need to re-enter it on each form.
personal.otherLastNamesPlaceholder: Maiden name, prior last name, etc.
personal.securityNote: Your SSN and date of birth are encrypted and used only for official tax form submission. They are never stored in plain text.

addressStep.title: Home Address
addressStep.desc: Your residential address is required for W-4, I-9, and state withholding. The state you select also determines which state withholding form applies to you.

w4.title: W-4: Federal Tax Withholding
w4.filingStatusDesc: Choose the status that best describes your situation. This is the most important field and affects every paycheck.
w4.multipleJobsDesc: Check this if you (or your spouse) hold more than one job simultaneously. This prevents under-withholding.
w4.dependentsDesc: Enter the number of qualifying children under 17 (line 3a, worth $2,000 each) and other dependents (line 3b, worth $500 each). Most employees leave both at 0.
w4.otherAdjustmentsDesc: Use these fields to fine-tune withholding for investment income, itemized deductions, or extra withholding per paycheck.
w4.exemptDesc: Only claim exempt if you owed $0 in federal tax last year and expect to owe $0 this year. Most employees do not qualify.
w4.multipleJobsLabel: Multiple jobs or spouse also works (Step 2)
w4.irsNote: For the most accurate withholding, use the IRS Withholding Estimator at

stateTax.title: State Tax Withholding
stateTax.noIncomeTaxDesc: No state withholding certificate is required. Click Continue to proceed.
stateTax.instructionsDesc: State withholding varies by state. Enter your filing status and allowances as shown on your state's withholding certificate.
stateTax.allowancesExplain: Each allowance reduces the amount withheld per paycheck. Claiming more allowances = less withheld; fewer = more withheld. Enter 0 if unsure — your state will apply the default.
stateTax.exemptExplain: Only claim exempt if you owed no state income tax last year and expect none this year.

i9.title: I-9: Employment Eligibility Verification
i9.section1Title: Section 1 — Employee (Complete by your first day)
i9.section1Desc: Federal law requires you to verify your identity and authorization to work in the U.S. You complete Section 1 now; your employer completes Section 2 within
i9.section1Suffix: of your first day of employment.
i9.lprDesc: Enter your Alien Registration Number (A-Number, starts with "A") in the field provided.
i9.alienDesc: Provide your A-Number, Form I-94 Admission Number, or foreign passport details as applicable.
i9.docsHeader: Documents you must present to your employer on your first day
listAItems: Foreign Passport with I-94 or I-551 stamp
listBCItems: B: Driver's license / State ID card
i9.docUploadDesc: Pre-upload copies of your identity documents. Physical originals must be presented to your employer on or before your first day of employment.
i9.listAChoiceDesc: One document — U.S. Passport, Permanent Resident Card, or Employment Authorization
i9.listBCChoiceDesc: Two documents — Identity (e.g., Driver's license) + Employment auth (e.g., SSN card)
i9.uploadListA: Click to upload List A document (PDF or image)
i9.uploadListB: Click to upload List B document (PDF or image)
i9.uploadListC: Click to upload List C document (PDF or image)
i9.preparerToggle: A preparer or translator helped me complete this form

deposit.title: Direct Deposit Authorization
deposit.desc: Authorize payroll direct deposit to your bank account. Your routing and account numbers can be found on a check or in your bank's mobile app.
deposit.uploadVoidedCheck: Click to upload a voided check (PDF or image)

ack.title: Acknowledgments
ack.desc: Review and acknowledge the following required policies to proceed to the document signing step.
ack.workersCompBody: I acknowledge that I have been informed of my rights under the workers' compensation laws of my state. I understand that workers' compensation insurance covers work-related injuries and illnesses, and I agree to report any such injury or illness to my supervisor immediately.
ack.workersCompToggle: I acknowledge the Workers' Compensation policy
ack.handbookBody: I acknowledge that I have received, read, and understood the Employee Handbook. I understand it is not a contract of employment, that it may be updated at any time, and that I agree to comply with all policies and procedures contained within.
ack.handbookToggle: I acknowledge receipt of the Employee Handbook
ack.submitNote: After clicking Submit, you'll be guided through electronically signing your W-4, state withholding form, and I-9 via DocuSign.

onboarding.securelyStored: Securely stored — leave blank to keep, or enter a new value to overwrite.

errors.uploadFailed: Failed to upload document. Please try again.
errors.voidedCheckFailed: Failed to upload voided check. Please try again.
errors.previewFailed: Could not load PDF preview. Please try again.
errors.signingFailed: Could not create signing session. Please try again.

account.payStubsSubtitle: View and download your pay stubs
account.taxDocumentsSubtitle: View and download your W-2s, 1099s, and other tax documents
account.kioskPinHint: Set a 4-8 digit PIN for kiosk/shop floor authentication.

communications.title: Communication Tracking
communications.subtitle: Connect mailboxes and phone systems so inbound and outbound messages auto-log against active leads and contacts.
communications.disconnectMessage: This will disconnect {{provider}} ({{account}}). You can reconnect later.
communications.capabilityDisabled: {{capability}} is not enabled on this install — admin must enable it before new connections can be added.

oauth.callbackPending: Exchanging the authorization code with the provider.
oauth.callbackSuccess: First sync runs within 15 minutes — returning you to Communications.
oauth.callbackFailed: The provider rejected the authorization. Please try again.
oauth.callbackMissingParams: The provider redirect was missing the expected code or state. Please retry.
oauth.callbackProviderUnknown: We lost track of which provider you were authorizing — please retry from Communications.

imap.title: Connect IMAP Mailbox
imap.pickPreset: Choose your email provider — host and port will be filled in for you.
imap.connectSuccess: IMAP mailbox connected — first sync runs within 15 minutes.
imap.connectFailed: Connection test failed. Verify host, port, and credentials.

account.colorThemeHint: Choose between light and dark mode.
account.fontScaleHint: Adjust the text size across the application. Changes take effect immediately.
account.ambientIdleTimeoutHint: How long to wait before automatically entering ambient (read-only) display mode after no activity.
account.taxFormsIntro: Complete the following forms to finish your onboarding. Click any form to review the document and sign digitally.
account.downloadFormInstruction: Download the form, complete it, then return here to acknowledge completion.
account.digitalSigningStart: Click below to start the digital signing process.
account.signedDocAvailable: Your signed document is available for download.
account.formPendingSetup: This form is being configured by your administrator. You will be able to fill it out electronically once setup is complete.
account.acknowledgmentInstruction: Review the form above, then click below to confirm you have read and completed it.
account.identityDocsInstruction: Upload identity documents to verify your eligibility. Examples: birth certificate, driver's license, social security card, passport.
account.formAlreadySubmitted: This form has already been submitted
account.formSensitiveNote: For security, previously submitted data is not displayed. You can submit a new version if your information has changed.
account.formCompletedNote: This form was completed successfully. You can submit a new version if your information has changed.
account.docListADesc: Documents that establish both identity and employment authorization
account.fillAndSignReady: This form is ready for digital signing.
account.identityDocNeedListB: You need a document from List B (identity)
account.identityDocNeedListC: You need a document from List C (employment authorization)
account.identityDocRequired: Identity documents are required for I-9 verification
account.chatNotificationsHint: Control sound, vibration, and popup behavior for incoming chat messages.

errors.notFound: The requested resource was not found.

errors.unableToReachServer: Unable to reach the server. Check your network connection.

integrations.qbDescription: Accounting, invoicing, and payment sync via Intuit

training.title: Training Library
training.walkthroughDesc: This is an interactive guided tour. Click the button to start, and the tour will walk you through the interface step by step.
training.trackingNote: Tour completion is tracked per-device via browser storage.

aiAssistants.deleteMessage: This will deactivate "{{name}}". This action cannot be undone for built-in assistants.

complianceTemplateDialog.uploadHint: Upload a PDF to override the auto-synced or linked document for this template.
complianceTemplateDialog.pdfFillConfigHint: Upload the blank government PDF and configure the AcroField map to enable automatic PDF fill before DocuSeal signing.
complianceTemplateDialog.acroFieldMapHint: JSON object mapping dynamic form field IDs to PDF AcroForm field names. Example: {"firstName": "topmostSubform[0].Page1[0].f1_01[0]"}

stateWithholdingDialog.title: State Tax Withholding Forms

stateWithholdingDialog.noStateTaxHint: State withholding form not required — the compliance requirement will be deactivated.

shared.irreversibleHint: Irreversible — jobs cannot be moved backward

fileUpload.dragAndDrop: Upload files — drag and drop or click to browse

notifications.soundDesc: Play a sound when a notification arrives

invoices.subtitle: Generate invoices and track billing status
invoices.accountingManaged: Invoices are managed by {{provider}}. Changes here are read-only.
invoices.accountingManagedDefault: Invoices are managed by your accounting provider. Changes here are read-only.
invoices.uninvoicedBanner: {{count}} completed job has not been invoiced.
invoices.uninvoicedBannerPlural: {{count}} completed jobs have not been invoiced.

invoices.deleteInvoiceMessage: Delete draft "{{number}}"? This action cannot be undone.

vendors.title: Vendors
vendors.subtitle: Manage suppliers and vendor relationships
vendors.noPurchaseOrders: No purchase orders for this vendor
vendors.deleteVendorMessage: This will remove "{{name}}" from the system. This action cannot be undone.

fork.title: New Vendor
fork.hint: Choose how to set up this vendor.
fork.quickDesc: Just the essentials — company name and basic contact. Flesh out the rest later.
fork.guidedDesc: Step-by-step for strategic or approved vendors — classification, address, terms, and the parts they supply.

guided.title: New Vendor — Guided Setup
guided.step1Hint: Who is this vendor and how do you reach them?
guided.step2Hint: What kind of vendor relationship is this? Optional — it tailors the rest of the setup.
guided.step3Hint: Remit-to / primary address. All fields optional.
guided.step4Hint: Payment terms and any notes about working with this vendor.
guided.step5Hint: Add the parts or consumables this vendor supplies. Optional — you can add them later from the vendor's Catalog tab.
guided.step6Hint: Confirm the details, then create the vendor.

type.TransactionalPlaybook: Keep it light — capture the basics and order as needed.
type.StrategicDesc: Key supplier worth investing in the relationship.
type.StrategicPlaybook: Capture the account manager and core capabilities so the team knows who to call.
type.SubcontractorDesc: Performs outsourced operations on your parts.
type.SubcontractorPlaybook: Record the processes they run and any certifications you rely on.
type.DistributorDesc: Resells parts made by other manufacturers.
type.DistributorPlaybook: Note the lines they carry so you know what to source through them.

detail.catalogEmpty: This vendor has no parts in the catalog yet.
detail.catalogEmptyHelp: Add a part this vendor supplies.

purchaseOrders.title: Purchase Orders
purchaseOrders.subtitle: Order materials and track vendor deliveries
purchaseOrders.cancelPoMessage: Cancel "{{number}}"? This action cannot be undone.
purchaseOrders.deletePoMessage: Delete draft "{{number}}"? This action cannot be undone.
purchaseOrders.vendorDeactivatedWarning: Vendor '{{name}}' is deactivated. Reactivate it or choose another.
purchaseOrders.shortCloseMessage: Closes this PO and marks the unreceived quantity as cancelled-not-received. Use when the remainder will not arrive (vendor backorder cancelled, item discontinued).
purchaseOrders.belowVendorMinimumMessage: PO total is below the vendor minimum order amount of ${{amount}}. The vendor may reject or surcharge this order.

offTier.title: Off-tier prices
offTier.intro: {{count}} line(s) priced more than {{threshold}}% off the current vendor tier. Pick how to handle each — by default the entered prices stay as one-off exceptions and don't change vendor master pricing.
offTier.updateTier: Update vendor's tiers with this price
offTier.updateSummary: {{count}} line(s) will insert a new vendor price tier; remaining off-tier lines stay as one-off exceptions on this PO only.
offTier.exceptionSummary: All off-tier prices recorded as one-off exceptions for this PO. Vendor master pricing unchanged.
offTier.updateTierFailed: Couldn't update one or more vendor tiers — PO will still be created.

recurring.title: Recurring Order Templates
recurring.hint: Templates the nightly Hangfire job spins into fresh sales orders. Each template carries a customer, an interval, and the lines to replicate.
recurring.empty: No recurring order templates yet. Create one to schedule auto-generated sales orders for a customer.
recurring.deleteMessage: Soft-delete "{{name}}"? Already-generated sales orders are kept; only future generations stop.

salesTax.deleteMessage: This tax rate will be permanently deleted.

maintenance.title: Predictive Maintenance
maintenance.hint: Open predictions need a decision: acknowledge, schedule preventive work, resolve, or mark false-positive. Resolved + false-positive close the loop and feed the model accuracy stats.
maintenance.empty: No predictions match the current filter. Lower-severity alerts may be hidden — try Status: All.

resolveDialog.resolveHint: Capture what happened. The notes feed model-accuracy reporting.
resolveDialog.falsePositiveHint: False positives count negatively against model accuracy — note why this one didn't match real failure conditions.

mrp.title: Material Requirements Planning
mrp.noPlannedOrders: No planned orders. Run MRP to generate.

runDialog.title: Run MRP
runDialog.hint: A Full run re-plans every part. Net Change only re-plans parts whose demand or supply has changed since the last full run — much faster on a populated install.
runDialog.hintSimulate: Simulation runs the full MRP engine but discards the planned orders + exceptions when the dialog closes. Use it to safely preview the impact of a planning-horizon change.
runDialog.horizonHint: How many days into the future MRP should plan supply for. 90 days is typical.

scheduleDialog.linesEmpty: No lines yet — add at least one part with a quantity and due date.

forecastDialog.title: Generate Demand Forecast
forecastDialog.hint: Pick a part with sales history and a forecast method. The engine reads `historical periods` of past demand and projects `forecast periods` into the future.
forecastDialog.alphaHint: Higher α weights recent observations more heavily. 0.3 is a common starting point.

runDetail.title: MRP Run
runDetail.pickPart: Select a part to view its time-bucket plan and demand pegging.

mpsActual.title: MPS vs Actual
mpsActual.hint: Per-part variance between scheduled quantity and completed production runs in the schedule's date window.
mpsActual.empty: No matching production runs found in this schedule's window.

login.title: Sign in to your portal

login.devLink: Dev mode — SMTP isn't configured. Use the link directly:

callback.missing: This link is missing its sign-in token. Please request a new one.

completeI9.reverificationNote: Leave blank for US citizens, permanent residents, and documents with no expiration. When set, a reminder will be sent 90 days before expiration.

announcements.title: Announcements
announcements.deleteTemplateMessage: Delete this template? This cannot be undone.

expenseSettings.title: Expense Policy
expenseSettings.intro: Configure thresholds, receipt requirements, and approval rules for expense submissions.
expenseSettings.allowSelfApprovalHint: When enabled, submitters may auto-approve their own expenses up to the threshold below.
expenseSettings.autoApproveThresholdHint: Expenses at or below this amount are approved automatically. Leave blank to require manual approval on every expense.
expenseSettings.maxAmountHint: Largest amount a single expense may claim. Leave blank for no upper limit.
expenseSettings.requireReceiptHint: When enabled, submitters must attach a receipt file before submitting any expense.
expenseSettings.minDescriptionLengthHint: Shortest acceptable expense description (in characters). Use 0 to allow blank descriptions.
expenseSettings.thresholdExceedsMax: Auto-approve threshold cannot exceed the maximum expense amount.

welcomePage.tagline: An open ERP for engineering shops.
welcomePage.lede: Armory Works Forge is a self-hostable shop-floor + business management platform — kanban, jobs, parts, inventory, purchasing, sales, payroll, and more — designed to run alongside your QuickBooks file or completely standalone.
welcomePage.caveat: This is a public demo. Data resets periodically and is shared with everyone signed in.

recentCommunications.title: Recent Communications
recentCommunications.empty: No recent communications. Connect a mailbox or phone line under Account > Communications to start auto-logging.

setupIntegrations.title: Set up your integrations
setupIntegrations.subtitle: Optional services that can be wired up now or later. Skip any you don't use — you can always come back to Admin → Integrations to set them up.
setupIntegrations.empty: No integrations are available on this install.
setupIntegrations.footerHint: You can revisit this anytime under Admin → Integrations.
setupIntegrations.loadFailed: Couldn't load integrations. Try refreshing or check the server logs.
